preamble: The transformation of the high front rounded vowel [y] (or the palatal glide [j]) into the voiced alveolar fricative [z] is documented through parallel phonetic shifts and patterns of uniformity within the sibilant class, where sounds move in tandem across the phonetic space.

examples.note: Assibilation of palatal glides in specific phonetic environments during the development of Middle and Modern Indo-Aryan varieties.

sources: Chodroff, E. (2021). Structured variation in the phonetic realization of American English sibilants.
Phonological research on sibilant class uniformity.
